title: Getting Started with shinybrain
text: analysis result contains project metadata plus internal tibbles used parser graph builder: project files contexts symbols references nodes edges issues
code: library(shinybrain)  app_dir <- system.file("examples", "basic_app", package = "shinybrain") if (app_dir == "") app_dir <- file.path("..", "inst", "examples", "basic_app") if (!dir.exists(app_dir)) app_dir <- file.path("inst", "examples", "basic_app")  result <- analyze_shiny_project(app_dir) names(result) #> [1] "project"    "files"      "contexts"   "symbols"    "references" #> [6] "nodes"      "edges"      "issues"

title: Getting Started with shinybrain
text: App Brain package’s portable reporting object. includes: project metadata file inventory graph nodes edges generated insights issue records export options

title: Getting Started with shinybrain
code: print_brain_console(brain) #>  #> ============================================================  #>   ShinyBrain Report   basic_app  #>   shinybrain  0.1.0    2026-04-24 00:45  UTC #> ============================================================  #>  #>   Complexity: Moderate ( 50 / 100 ) [██████████░░░░░░░░░░]  #>   Chain depth: 5  ⚠  deep pipeline  #>  #>   At a Glance  #>   -----------  #>   Files         : 2  #>   Contexts      : 13  #>   Nodes         : 18  #>   Edges         : 13  #>   Unresolved    : 0  #>   Node types    : helper_fn × 2   input × 5   observer × 3   output × 3   reactive × 3   state × 2   #>  #>   Files  #>   -----  #>   ✓ app.R ( entry , 88 lines ) #>   ✓ helpers.R ( sourced , 32 lines ) #>  #>   Reactive Graph  #>   --------------  #>   [ state ] #>     • saved   → used by 1   #>     • counters    #>   [ reactive ] #>     • dataset   → used by 1   #>     • filtered   → used by 2   #>     • summary_data   → used by 2   #>   [ output ] #>     • output$status   → used by 1   #>     • output$preview   → used by 1   #>     • output$chart   → used by 1   #>   [ observer ] #>     • observeEvent_46   → used by 2   #>     • observeEvent_54    #>     • observe_60    #>   [ helper_fn ] #>     • filter_by_threshold   → used by 1   #>     • summarize_numeric   → used by 1   #>   [ input ] #>     • input$dataset    #>     • input$cutoff    #>     • input$go_btn    #>     • input$save_btn    #>     • input$reset_btn    #>  #>   Insights  #>   --------  #>   ⚠️  [WARNING] 'write.csv()' inside 'observeEvent_46' is a side effect #>      not wrapped in isolate(); it will re-run on every reactive #>      invalidation cycle.  #>  #> ============================================================  #>  #>   Run shinybrain_report(..., format = "html") for the interactive report.

Getting Started with shinybrain
text: already know app entry file, use analyze_shiny_file():

title: Getting Started with shinybrain
text: See vignette("shinybrain-workflows") tour included example apps typical analysis workflows.

title: Workflows and Included Examples
text: happy-path example. includes: reactive() eventReactive() observeEvent() observe() reactiveVal() reactiveValues() renderText() renderTable() renderPlot() sourced helper functions side-effect calls write.csv() message()
code: library(shinybrain)  basic_app <- system.file("examples", "basic_app", package = "shinybrain") if (basic_app == "") basic_app <- file.path("..", "inst", "examples", "basic_app") if (!dir.exists(basic_app)) basic_app <- file.path("inst", "examples", "basic_app")  basic_result <- analyze_shiny_project(basic_app) table(basic_result$contexts$context_type) #>  #> event_reactive      helper_fn  observe_event       observer  output_render  #>              1              2              2              1              3  #>       reactive      state_val   state_values  #>              2              1              1

title: Workflows and Included Examples
text: app designed analyzer edge cases rather runtime execution. includes: resolved source("helpers.R") missing source target dynamic source() call dynamic input[[...]] lookup
code: edge_app <- system.file("examples", "edge_case_app", package = "shinybrain") if (edge_app == "") edge_app <- file.path("..", "inst", "examples", "edge_case_app") if (!dir.exists(edge_app)) edge_app <- file.path("inst", "examples", "edge_case_app")  edge_result <- analyze_shiny_project(edge_app) edge_result$issues[, c("severity", "issue_type")] #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   severity issue_type          #>   <chr>    <chr>               #> 1 warning  missing_file        #> 2 warning  unsupported_pattern

title: Workflows and Included Examples
text: tests/fixtures/ directory contains small focused projects regression testing: basic_single_file observer_and_state source_chain useful want compact reproducible inputs working parser graph builder.

title: Workflows and Included Examples
text: brain object, usual downstream workflow :
code: brain <- build_brain(basic_result)  export_brain_json(brain, "brain.json") export_brain_markdown(brain, "brain.md") export_brain_html(brain, "brain.html", open = FALSE)

title: Workflows and Included Examples
text: repository includes convenience runner: analyzes bundled example apps, prints summaries, writes export artifacts temporary directory.

text: ShinyBrain Authors. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Authors S (2026). shinybrain: Static Analysis App Brain Export Shiny Applications. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/PrigasG/shinybrain.
code: @Manual{,   title = {shinybrain: Static Analysis and App Brain Export for Shiny Applications},   author = {ShinyBrain Authors},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/PrigasG/shinybrain}, }

title: shinybrain
text: shinybrain statically analyzes Shiny applications without running . parses app source code, identifies reactive contexts helper functions, resolves references across sourced files, builds portable “App Brain” can exported structured JSON, Markdown, HTML dependency report.

title: shinybrain
text: project-level parse Shiny code without launching app Reactive graph nodes edges inputs, reactives, outputs, observers, helpers, state Detection sourced files, missing source() targets, dynamic patterns resolved statically Export formats can hand humans, CI jobs, LLM workflows

title: shinybrain
text: install local clone:

title: shinybrain
text: can inspect graph console: export downstream use:
code: library(shinybrain)  app_dir <- system.file("examples", "basic_app", package = "shinybrain") if (app_dir == "") app_dir <- file.path("..", "inst", "examples", "basic_app") if (!dir.exists(app_dir)) app_dir <- file.path("inst", "examples", "basic_app")  result <- analyze_shiny_project(app_dir) brain  <- build_brain(result)  brain$summary #> $n_files #> [1] 2 #>  #> $n_contexts #> [1] 13 #>  #> $n_nodes #> [1] 18 #>  #> $n_edges #> [1] 13 #>  #> $n_symbols #> [1] 18 #>  #> $n_unresolved #> [1] 0 #>  #> $n_issues #> [1] 4 #>  #> $n_insights #> [1] 1 #>  #> $max_chain_depth #> [1] 5 #>  #> $complexity #> $complexity$score #> [1] 50 #>  #> $complexity$label #> [1] "Moderate" #>  #>  #> $node_breakdown #> $node_breakdown$helper_fn #> [1] 2 #>  #> $node_breakdown$input #> [1] 5 #>  #> $node_breakdown$observer #> [1] 3 #>  #> $node_breakdown$output #> [1] 3 #>  #> $node_breakdown$reactive #> [1] 3 #>  #> $node_breakdown$state #> [1] 2 #>  #>  #> $edge_breakdown #> $edge_breakdown$calls #> [1] 2 #>  #> $edge_breakdown$depends_on #> [1] 4 #>  #> $edge_breakdown$feeds_into #> [1] 5 #>  #> $edge_breakdown$reads_state #> [1] 1 #>  #> $edge_breakdown$writes_state #> [1] 1 print_brain_console(brain) #>  #> ============================================================  #>   ShinyBrain Report   basic_app  #>   shinybrain  0.1.0    2026-04-24 00:43  UTC #> ============================================================  #>  #>   Complexity: Moderate ( 50 / 100 ) [██████████░░░░░░░░░░]  #>   Chain depth: 5  ⚠  deep pipeline  #>  #>   At a Glance  #>   -----------  #>   Files         : 2  #>   Contexts      : 13  #>   Nodes         : 18  #>   Edges         : 13  #>   Unresolved    : 0  #>   Node types    : helper_fn × 2   input × 5   observer × 3   output × 3   reactive × 3   state × 2   #>  #>   Files  #>   -----  #>   ✓ app.R ( entry , 88 lines ) #>   ✓ helpers.R ( sourced , 32 lines ) #>  #>   Reactive Graph  #>   --------------  #>   [ state ] #>     • saved   → used by 1   #>     • counters    #>   [ reactive ] #>     • dataset   → used by 1   #>     • filtered   → used by 2   #>     • summary_data   → used by 2   #>   [ output ] #>     • output$status   → used by 1   #>     • output$preview   → used by 1   #>     • output$chart   → used by 1   #>   [ observer ] #>     • observeEvent_46   → used by 2   #>     • observeEvent_54    #>     • observe_60    #>   [ helper_fn ] #>     • filter_by_threshold   → used by 1   #>     • summarize_numeric   → used by 1   #>   [ input ] #>     • input$dataset    #>     • input$cutoff    #>     • input$go_btn    #>     • input$save_btn    #>     • input$reset_btn    #>  #>   Insights  #>   --------  #>   ⚠️  [WARNING] 'write.csv()' inside 'observeEvent_46' is a side effect #>      not wrapped in isolate(); it will re-run on every reactive #>      invalidation cycle.  #>  #> ============================================================  #>  #>   Run shinybrain_report(..., format = "html") for the interactive report. export_brain_json(brain, "brain.json") export_brain_markdown(brain, "brain.md") export_brain_html(brain, "brain.html", open = FALSE)

title: shinybrain
text: package ships concrete example apps inst/examples/, available installation via system.file("examples", ..., package = "shinybrain"): basic_app/: runnable Shiny app covering happy path edge_case_app/: analyzer fixture missing source() target, dynamic source() call, dynamic input[[...]] access test fixtures tests/fixtures/ also include compact examples single-file apps, observer/state behavior, sourced-file projects.

title: Brain export options — brain_options
code: brain_options(   include_snippets = TRUE,   snippet_max_lines = 15L,   include_file_paths = "relative",   include_unresolved = TRUE,   strict_missing_sources = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Brain export options — brain_options
text: include_snippets Logical. Include code snippets node data. snippet_max_lines Integer. Max lines per snippet. include_file_paths One "relative", "basename", "none". include_unresolved Logical. Include unresolved references export. strict_missing_sources Logical. TRUE, elevates missing-file warnings (unresolved source() targets) errors. ghost node still emitted either way. verbose Logical. TRUE, build_brain() prints diagnostic report every state-related reference (state_read, state_write, ambiguous state calls) target_arg_count. Useful state edge expected missing graph.

title: Brain export options — brain_options
text: Named list options

title: shinybrain 0.1.0
text: Initial public release shinybrain. Added static analysis single-file multi-file Shiny projects. Added source-chain resolution, missing-source detection, ghost-node support. Added graph construction inputs, reactives, outputs, observers, helpers, state. Added JSON, Markdown, HTML App Brain exports. Added example apps, vignettes, pkgdown configuration, CRAN submission materials.
